title: Sirt1 transgene delivery improves diabetes-impaired wound healing.
snippet: Diabetes mellitus severely impairs wound healing, in part due to persistent inflammation, impaired angiogenesis, and dysregulated cellular responses. Sirtuin-1 (Sirt1), an NAD-dependent deacetylase, has emerged as a promising therapeutic target for restoring regenerative capacity in diabetic wounds. However, effective and localized gene delivery remains a m...
